marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: U1 (Theory 10, Practical 10), U2 (Theory 45, Practical 60), U3 (Theory 15, Practical -)
Class XII: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: U1 (Theory 40, Practical 50), U2 (Theory 10, Practical -), U3 (Theory 20, Practical 20)
question_paper_design: Class XI and XII Theory papers based on unit weightage distribution provided in the syllabus.
Practical exams include Lab Tests, Report Files with Viva Voce.
competency_weightage: Competencies assessed through problem-solving steps (analyzing, algorithm development), coding, testing, debugging, and application of concepts like file handling, SQL connectivity, and network protocols.
Weightage reflects unit marks distribution: Computational Thinking/Programming carries the highest weight in both classes.
bloom_levels: Assessment covers understanding (explain cyber ethics), applying (write Python programs for specific tasks), analyzing (debugging errors), creating (project work, generating patterns).
Specific Bloom levels inferred from practical requirements: Logic application (60%), Documentation (20%), Code Quality (20%).
internal_assessment: Class XI Lab Test (12 marks): Python program evaluation.
Class XII Lab Test (8 marks for Class XII, 4 marks SQL queries).
Viva Voce included in practical assessment.
practical_assessment: Class XI: Report File with minimum 20 Python programs and Viva Voce.
Class XII: Report File with minimum 15 Python programs, 5 sets of SQL queries (one/two tables), and 4 programs on Python-SQL connectivity.
project_assessment: Class XI Project: Uses concepts learnt; marks allocated in practical section.
Class XII Project: Tangible/useful application using Python file handling or Python-SQL connectivity. Group size (2-3 students). Marks allocation included in practical total.
